root_text.sn48.44:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbakoṭṭhake.
root_text.sn48.44:1.3: Tatra kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn48.44:1.5: saddhindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānan”ti?
root_text.sn48.44:2.1: “Na khvāhaṁ ettha, bhante, bhagavato saddhāya gacchāmi—
root_text.sn48.44:2.2: saddhindriyaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.44:2.3: Yesañhetaṁ, bhante, aññātaṁ assa adiṭṭhaṁ aviditaṁ asacchikataṁ aphassitaṁ paññāya, te tattha paresaṁ saddhāya gaccheyyuṁ—
root_text.sn48.44:2.4: saddhindriyaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.44:2.5: Yesañca kho etaṁ, bhante, ñātaṁ diṭṭhaṁ viditaṁ sacchikataṁ phassitaṁ paññāya, nikkaṅkhā te tattha nibbicikicchā—
root_text.sn48.44:2.6: saddhindriyaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.44:2.7: Mayhañca kho etaṁ, bhante, ñātaṁ diṭṭhaṁ viditaṁ sacchikataṁ phassitaṁ paññāya.
root_text.sn48.44:2.8: Nikkaṅkhvāhaṁ tattha nibbicikiccho saddhindriyaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānan”ti.
root_text.sn48.44:3.2: Yesañhetaṁ, sāriputta, aññātaṁ assa adiṭṭhaṁ aviditaṁ asacchikataṁ aphassitaṁ paññāya, te tattha paresaṁ saddhāya gaccheyyuṁ—
root_text.sn48.44:3.3: saddhindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.44:3.4: Yesañca kho etaṁ, sāriputta, ñātaṁ diṭṭhaṁ viditaṁ sacchikataṁ phassitaṁ paññāya, nikkaṅkhā te tattha nibbicikicchā—
root_text.sn48.44:3.5: saddhindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānan”ti.

translation_text.sn48.44:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in Sāvatthī at the eastern gate.
translation_text.sn48.44:1.3: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Sāriputta:
translation_text.sn48.44:1.5: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, when developed and cultivated, have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination?”
translation_text.sn48.44:2.1: “Sir, in this case I don’t rely on faith in the Buddha’s claim that
translation_text.sn48.44:2.2: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, when developed and cultivated, have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.
translation_text.sn48.44:2.3: There are those who have not known or seen or understood or realized or experienced this with wisdom. They may rely on faith in this matter.
translation_text.sn48.44:2.5: But there are those who have known, seen, understood, realized, and experienced this with wisdom. They have no doubts or uncertainties in this matter.
translation_text.sn48.44:2.7: I have known, seen, understood, realized, and experienced this with wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.44:2.8: I have no doubts or uncertainties that the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, when developed and cultivated, have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.”
translation_text.sn48.44:3.2: There are those who have not known or seen or understood or realized or experienced this with wisdom. They may rely on faith in this matter.
translation_text.sn48.44:3.4: But there are those who have known, seen, understood, realized, and experienced this with wisdom. They have no doubts or uncertainties that
translation_text.sn48.44:3.5: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, when developed and cultivated, have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.”

variant_text.sn48.44:2.3: aphassitaṁ → apassitaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, mr); aphusitaṁ (pts1ed)
